name: GitHub
tagline: Source control, issues, pull requests, releases, actions.
security: Personal access tokens (PATs) are user-scoped — prefer GitHub Apps for production integrations.
Verify webhook signatures with the X-Hub-Signature-256 header and your GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
Use fine-grained PATs over classic PATs — scope them to specific repos and permissions.
Never log webhook payloads that include code diffs — they may contain secrets pushed by users.

patterns.name: PR notifier
patterns.description: Listen to pull_request events and post summaries to Slack/Discord.

patterns.name: Issue triage automation
patterns.description: Auto-label, assign, or close issues based on content via webhooks.

patterns.name: Release publisher
patterns.description: On release.published, push artifacts or notify external systems.

triggers.name: Push to repository
triggers.description: Fires on any push to any branch.

triggers.name: Pull request opened
triggers.description: Fires when a new PR is opened.

triggers.name: Pull request merged
triggers.description: Fires when a PR is merged into its base branch.

triggers.name: Issue opened
triggers.description: Fires when a new issue is created.

triggers.name: Issue closed
triggers.description: Fires when an issue is closed.

triggers.name: Release published
triggers.description: Fires when a release transitions from draft to published.

triggers.name: Workflow run completed
triggers.description: Fires when a GitHub Actions workflow run finishes (success or failure).

actions.name: Create issue
actions.description: Open a new issue in a repository.

actions.name: Comment on issue or PR
actions.description: Add a comment to an existing issue or pull request.

actions.name: Create pull request
actions.description: Open a new PR from a branch.

actions.name: Create release
actions.description: Publish a new release with optional assets.

actions.name: Dispatch workflow
actions.description: Manually trigger a workflow_dispatch GitHub Actions workflow.

auth.description: Personal access token or app installation token used as Authorization: Bearer.
GitHub App installation — preferred for production multi-tenant integrations.
HMAC secret used to verify webhook deliveries.
